data.title: Insurer Humana prices $1B junior subordinated notes offering due 2056
data.description:
• Humana (HUM) priced a public offering of $1 billion in aggregate principal amount of fixed-to-fixed rate junior subordinated notes due 2056 at 100.000 percent of the principal amount. The junior subordinated notes offering is expected to close on March 9, 2026.
  • The health insurance company expects net proceeds from the junior subordinated notes offering to be approximately $986 million and intends to use them for general corporate purposes, which may include the repayment of existing indebtedness, including borrowings under its commercial paper program.

data.title: Waste Connections prices $600M debt offering via issuance of senior notes
Baker Hughes prices $6.5B and €3B dual-currency debt offering
Trump says Iran wants deal; Tehran says it is not seeking ceasefire, warns U.S. invasion would be ‘big disaster’
data.description:
Iran’s foreign minister said Thursday that the country is “not asking for a ceasefire” with the United States and Israel and sees no reason to enter negotiations after nearly a week of fighting. Araghchi, in an exclusive interview with NBC News, said that Iran had not made a decision to start a war with its neighbors.

"We have not attacked our neighbors. We have not attacked Muslim countries," Araghchi said. "We have attacked American targets and American bases, American installations, which are unfortunately located in the soils of our neighbors."

"There is no way that they can win this war. As long as it takes, we resist," he said. "So they better stop this war and stop killing our people." He said it would be “a big disaster for them” if the U.S. military invaded Iran.

The comments come as President Donald Trump on Thursday said Tehran was reaching out to ​the United States about making a deal amid ‌U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran, adding that further action to reduce pressure on oil was imminent.

"They're calling, they're saying 'how do we ​make a deal?' I said you're being ​a little bit late," said Trump, speaking at ⁠an event with the Inter Miami soccer team ​at the White House.

U.S. crude oil futures (CL1:COM) fell 1.2% to $80.04/bbl, and Brent crude (CO1:COM) was down about 1% to $84.59/bbl at press time.

data.title: Friday’s Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Employment Situation

The most closely watched of all economic indicators, the employment situation is a set of monthly labor market indicators based on two separate reports: the establishment survey which tracks 650,000 worksites and offers the nonfarm payroll and average hourly earnings headlines and the household survey which interviews 60,000 households and generates the unemployment rate.

Jobs expected up 60K, a moderate showing these days, with the jobless rate up a tick at 4.4 percent versus 4.3 percent in January.

8:30 AM Retail Sales

Retail sales measure the total receipts at stores that sell merchandise and related services to final consumers.

Sales are expected to be basically flat, up 0.1 percent, ex-autos. Not impressive, especially as these are nominal numbers. 

10:00 AM Business Inventories 

Business inventories are the dollar amount of inventories held by manufacturers, wholesalers, and retailers.

Inventories seen flat in December after edging up 0.1 percent in November.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.

1:30 PM Beth Hammack Speaks

Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack participates in "On the Safe-haven Status of the Dollar" panel before the 2026 U.S. Monetary Policy Forum sponsored by the University of Chicago Booth School of Business.

3:00 PM Consumer Credit

The dollar value of consumer installment credit outstanding. Changes in consumer credit indicate the state of consumer finances and portend future spending patterns. 

A trend like a rise of $11.1 billion in the call after an unusually large $24 billion increase in the previous month.

data.title: Nutex Health reports Q4 results; authorizes stock repurchase program of up to $25M
data.description:
• Nutex Health press release (NUTX): Q4 Revenue of $151.7M (-41.1% Y/Y) misses by $107.12M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA attributable to Nutex Health of $16.6 million, as compared to Adjusted EBITDA attributable to Nutex Health of $86.7 million for the three months ended December 31, 2024, a decrease of 80.8%.
  • Total visits at the Hospital Division were 48,205 for the three months ended December 31, 2025, as compared to 45,444 for the same period in 2024, an increase of 2,761 or 6.1%. Visits at mature hospitals decreased by 0.3% in the three months ended December 31, 2025 as compared to the same period in 2024.
  • Net cash from operating activities of $70.4 million for the three months ended December 31, 2025, as compared to $0.1 million for the same period in 2024.
  • The board of directors has authorized a second stock repurchase program of up to $25.0 million of the company's common stock over the next six months.

data.title: Four Corners Property Trust declares $0.3665 dividend
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (FCPT) declares $0.3665/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 5.75%
  • Payable April 15; for shareholders of record March 31; ex-div March 31.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.3665 for two consecutive quarters.
  • See FCPT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: SoftBank seeks up to $40B loan to fund OpenAI investment, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
• SoftBank Group (SFTBF) (SFTBY) is seeking a loan of as much as $40 billion to mostly help finance its investment in U.S. tech giant OpenAI (OPENAI), people familiar with the matter told Bloomberg News. The transaction would be its largest-ever borrowing denominated solely in dollars.
  • The bridge loan would have a tenor of about 12 months, according to some of the people, who asked not to be identified discussing private matters. Four lenders, including JPMorgan Chase & Co (JPM) will be underwriting the facility, the report said.
  • The ChatGPT-maker last week confirmed that it has raised an astonishing $110B in its latest funding round, with $50B coming from Amazon (AMZN) and $30B apiece from Nvidia (NVDA) and SoftBank (SFTBY). Upon completion of the funding round, SoftBank now owns approximately $64.6 billion worth of OpenAI, or roughly 13% of the company.

data.title: Costco outlines 30-plus annual warehouse target while expanding digital and pricing strategies
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) Q2 2026

Management View

  • CEO Ron Vachris addressed the evolving tariff environment, stating the company is "reducing the impact of tariff on prices for our members" and leveraging strategies such as moving production, consolidating global buying, and increasing Kirkland Signature sourcing. Vachris indicated, "Our buyers continue to act with great agility and urgency, always with the goal of reducing the impact of tariff on prices for our members."
  • Vachris highlighted ongoing price reductions: "During the second quarter, we lowered prices on key items such as eggs, cheese, coffee and some paper products as we saw lower inflation in these commodities."
  • On growth, Vachris shared, "We opened 4 warehouses, including 1 relocation in the U.S., 1 net new U.S. location and 2 additional Canadian business centers. This brings our total warehouse count to 924 warehouses worldwide. We currently expect to have 28 net new openings in fiscal year '26 and are targeting 30-plus new openings per year in the coming years."
  • Vachris reported digital improvements with member experience enhancements, including "mobile wallet enhancements, pharmacy pay ahead and the rollout of employee pre-scan technology," and the piloting of automated pay stations with 8-second average transaction times.
  • CFO Gary Millerchip stated, "Net income for the second quarter came in at $2.035 billion or $4.58 per diluted share up nearly 14% from $1.788 billion or $4.02 per diluted share in the second quarter last year. Net sales for the second quarter were $68.24 billion, an increase of 9.1% from $62.53 billion in Q2 2025."
  • Millerchip also noted, "We reported membership fee income of $1.355 billion, an increase of $162 million or 13.6% year-over-year."

Outlook

  • Vachris reaffirmed the company's focus on expansion, stating, "We currently expect to have 28 net new openings in fiscal year '26 and are targeting 30-plus new openings per year in the coming years."
  • Millerchip provided guidance on capital expenditures: "We estimate CapEx for the full year will be approximately $6.5 billion as we continue to invest in building a larger pipeline of new warehouses, remodeling our existing warehouses to drive continued growth in high-volume buildings, expanding our depot network to support operations and enhancing the member digital experience."

Financial Results

  • Comparable sales were up 7.4% or 6.7% adjusted for gas price deflation and FX. Digitally-enabled comparable sales rose 22.6% or 21.7% adjusted for FX.
  • Traffic increased 3.1% worldwide and average transaction rose 4.2% worldwide.
  • Membership base expanded with 40.4 million paid executive memberships (up 9.5% year-over-year) and 82.1 million total paid members (up 4.8%).
  • U.S. and Canada renewal rate was 92.1%, down 10 basis points from last quarter. Worldwide renewal rate was 89.7%, unchanged.
  • Gross margin was higher year-over-year by 17 basis points, with a reported rate of 11.02%. Core-on-core margins were higher by 22 basis points.
  • Capital expenditure in Q2 was $1.29 billion.

Q&A

  • Christopher Horvers, JPMorgan: Asked about weather impacts and gold volatility. Millerchip responded, "Weather...created some volatility during the first 2 months of the year, but we wouldn't really call anything out...our traffic visits were a little bit lighter in the U.S. in February...but beyond that, I don't think there's anything that we would say that you should -- we'd look at in our results and say it was a major impact that should be adjusted for."
  • Michael Lasser, UBS: Inquired about digital enhancements and efficiency savings. Vachris said, "The digital enhancements we're making both online and in the warehouse have all been very beneficial for us...improving the member experience and it's making the throughput much better...we feel that we still get the same return from our members as we continue to invest in the business."
  • Charles Grom, Gordon Haskett: Asked about spring/summer assortment and gas price impacts. Vachris responded, "We're going back a little more traditional than we've seen last year. The supply chain has balanced out." Millerchip added, "About half of members that will shop at the gas station will also cross shop at the warehouse."
  • Simeon Gutman, Morgan Stanley: Questioned membership impact from competitor openings and digital opportunities. Vachris stated, "We don't see a negative effect, but we don't see the big tailwind we saw with new sign-ups as we would in the new market as well."
  • John Heinbockel, Guggenheim: Asked about Canada AUVs and international pipeline. Vachris described "a very good path of expansion in Canada over the next 5 years," and positive international prospects.
  • Other analyst questions covered real estate strategy, core margins, membership growth, renewal rates, inflation, digital advertising, cash deployment, pharmacy, and China expansion, with management emphasizing stability, investment, and targeted engagement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a generally neutral to slightly positive tone, with questions focused on operational details, digital innovation, margin trajectories, and international expansion. Concerns about weather, membership growth, and renewal rates were addressed with measured responses.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks and Q&A. Vachris and Millerchip consistently emphasized agility, investment in member value, and long-term growth. Phrases like "we feel good," "encouraged," and "our goal is" signaled ongoing confidence in strategy.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment remained stable, with both management and analysts displaying consistent focus on growth, efficiency, and digital advancement.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 28 net new warehouse openings in fiscal 2026 and a longer-term plan for 30-plus annual openings were reiterated, consistent with last quarter's message, though Q2 featured more discussion of creative real estate approaches.
  • Digital innovation progress continued, with additional personalization and automation updates compared to Q1.
  • Membership growth rate was slightly lower (4.8% vs. 5.2% last quarter), attributed to fewer new market openings and cycling of strong prior sign-ups.
  • U.S. and Canada renewal rate declined by 10 basis points, mirroring a similar drop last quarter; global rate remains flat.
  • Gross margin improvement was more pronounced in Q2, with 17 basis points reported vs. 4 basis points in Q1.
  • Management's tone remained confident and focused on sustainable growth and investment.

Risks and Concerns

  • Vachris highlighted ongoing tariff uncertainty and its impact on pricing, stating the company will "be transparent in how we plan to do this, if and when we receive any refunds."
  • Millerchip noted potential risks from Middle East instability affecting "fuel costs and shipping schedules if there is instability in the region for a sustained period of time."
  • Egg price deflation is expected to continue as a headwind to food and sundries sales.
  • Slight decline in U.S. and Canada renewal rates, attributed to higher penetration of online sign-ups, remains an area of focus.

Final Takeaway

Costco management signaled ongoing commitment to price leadership, membership value, and digital innovation, with robust expansion plans targeting 30-plus new warehouses annually. Operational improvements, digital enhancements, and strong execution in merchandising and membership engagement were highlighted as key drivers for continued growth, while management remains vigilant on tariffs, supply chain stability, and evolving consumer patterns.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
